sanskrit: ॐ इत्येकाक्षरं ब्रह्म व्याहरन् मामनुस्मरन्। यः प्रयाति त्यजन्देहं स याति परमां गतिम्॥
transliteration: oṃ ity ekākṣaraṃ brahma vyāharan mām anusmaran, yaḥ prayāti tyajan dehaṃ sa yāti paramāṃ gatim
literal_meaning: Uttering the single syllable 'Om' (the imperishable Brahman), while remembering Me, whoever departs by relinquishing the body, he attains the supreme goal.
contextual_meaning: Consciously anchoring the mind to a sacred symbol (Om) and a relational image of the Divine at the moment of transition dissolves death-anxiety and directs the psyche toward its highest potential.
psychological_state: A calm, focused, death-transcending awareness achieved by coupling mantra with personalized theistic remembrance.
trigger_scenario: A person in palliative care repeating their spiritual phrase while visualizing a loving deity, turning fear of the unknown into peaceful surrender.
anchor_text: Chant Om and hold the Divine in your heart as you breathe your last.
